senses.definition: an animal such as a dog or cat that we keep and care for at home

examples.x: In the pet store, you can find various types of pets, such as birds, rodents, and reptiles.
Jane's pet is a fluffy and playful golden retriever.
John wants to adopt a pet from the animal shelter.
Mark's pet, a colorful parrot, loves to mimic his laughter.
My friend has multiple pets, including a dog, a bird, and a cat.

senses.definition: a young sheep, especially one that is under one year

examples.x: Mark's family owns a farm with several lambs.
My father showed me a group of lambs playing together on the hillside.
Sarah likes to watch the playful lambs in the springtime.
The farmer took care of the newborn lambs in the barn.
We saw a cute lamb grazing in the meadow.

senses.definition: a large desert animal with a long neck and one or two humps on its back

examples.x: In some countries, camels are also raised for their milk and meat.
In the desert, camels are often used as a means of transportation.
John read about camels' ability to go long periods without water.
My son was fascinated by the unique hump on the camel's back.
The guide explained how camels have adapted to harsh desert conditions.

senses.definition: a type of large and wild animal that is from the cat family, has orange fur and black stripes, and is mostly found in Asia

examples.x: John learned that tigers are excellent swimmers.
Mark was amazed to see a tiger at the zoo.
The tiger is a majestic and powerful wild animal.
The tiger's orange and black stripes are distinctive.
Tigers are known for their hunting and stalking skills.

senses.definition: a very large animal that lives in the sea, with horizontal tail fin and a blowhole on top of its head for breathing

examples.x: During the nature cruise, they encountered a pod of playful dolphins and whales.
Jane saw a majestic whale breach out of the water while on a boat tour.
Sarah read a book about different species of whales.
The scientist conducted research on the communication patterns of whales.
The whale's massive tail fin is called a fluke.

senses.definition: a large black-and-white seabird that lives in the Antarctic, and can not fly but uses its wings for swimming

examples.x: During their trip to the aquarium, they witnessed penguins diving gracefully in the water.
Penguins are birds that cannot fly but are excellent swimmers.
Sarah watched a documentary about penguins in Antarctica.
The little girl giggled as the penguin waddled on the ice.
The penguin's black and white feathers provide camouflage in the water.

senses.definition: ‌a large sea fish with a pointed fin on its back and very sharp teeth

examples.x: During the boat tour, they saw a group of sharks feeding on fish.
John learned that some sharks can detect prey using electrical signals.
Mark watched a documentary about different species of sharks.
The shark is a fearsome predator of the ocean.
The shark's sharp teeth help it catch and eat its prey.

senses.definition: a small creature such as a bee or ant that has six legs, and generally one or two pairs of wings

examples.x: I was observing an insect crawling on the tree bark.
Insects have six legs and a segmented body.
Mark used a magnifying glass to examine the intricate details of the insect.
Sarah was amazed by the intricate patterns on the insect's wings.
The butterfly is a colorful and beautiful insect.

senses.definition: a small flying insect that has two wings

examples.x: Jane saw a fly land on the fruit and decided not to eat it.
John observed a colorful fly resting on a flower.
The fly quickly darted away when Jane tried to catch it.

senses.definition: a small creature that spins webs to catch insects for food, with eight legs and two fangs by which poison is injected to its prey

examples.x: I learned that spiders help control insect populations in the garden.
My sister is really afraid of spiders and prefers to stay away from them.
Sarah read a book about different types of spiders found around the world.
The children watched a spider crawl up the wall with curiosity.
The spider's web glistened in the sunlight, catching small insects.

senses.definition: a large reptile with very big jaws, sharp teeth, short legs, and a hard skin and long tail that lives in rivers and lakes in warmer regions

examples.x: I didn't know that crocodiles are excellent swimmers.
I watched a documentary about the life of crocodiles in the wild.
Mark saw a crocodile basking in the sun near the riverbank.
The crocodile silently approached its prey in the water.
The tour guide warned everyone to keep a safe distance from the crocodile.

senses.definition: an intelligent sea mammal that looks like a whale and has a long snout and teeth

examples.x: Mark was thrilled to see dolphins leaping out of the water during the boat tour.
Sarah enjoys watching dolphins swim gracefully in the ocean.
Sarah's dream is to swim with dolphins in their natural habitat.
The dolphins' sleek bodies allow them to glide effortlessly through the waves.
The trainer at the aquarium taught the dolphins to perform tricks.

senses.definition: a small to medium-sized carnivorous mammal with a pointed muzzle and bushy tail, often have reddish-brown fur and are known for being clever and adaptable

examples.x: Jane read a storybook about a curious fox and its adventures.
Sarah learned that foxes are clever and resourceful animals.
The farmer installed a fence to protect his chickens from foxes.
The fox's bushy tail helps it maintain balance while running.

senses.definition: a small animal of the rodent family, similar to a mouse, with a short tail and large cheeks for storing food

examples.x: His pet hamster loves to run on its exercise wheel.
My hamster has a habit of stuffing its cheeks with food.
My little son learned that hamsters are nocturnal animals.
The children built a cozy nest for their hamster in its cage.
The hamster's fur is soft and fluffy to touch.

senses.definition: the part of the body of an animal, a bird or a fish that sticks out at the back, which can move

examples.x: My cat has a long and bushy tail.
My friend's lizard can detach its tail when threatened.
The kangaroo uses its tail for balance while hopping.
The monkey's tail helps it swing from tree to tree.

senses.definition: the soft and thick hair that grows on the body of sheep and goats

examples.x: His sweater was made of luxurious wool that kept him cozy.
Mark's grandmother knitted a warm scarf using soft wool.
Sarah's favorite winter coat was made of high-quality wool.
The sheep's wool is naturally insulating, making it ideal for cold climates.
The soft wool from the sheep was used to make warm blankets.

senses.definition: an oval or rounded object laid by birds, reptiles, fish, or certain invertebrates as part of their reproductive process

examples.x: The chicken laid a fresh egg in the nesting box.

senses.definition: a net of thin threads made by a spider to catch insects for food

examples.x: After a rainstorm, the spider repaired its damaged web with great precision.
As I observed the delicate web, I marveled at the spider's skill in crafting such a masterpiece.
I accidentally walked through a spider's web while hiking in the forest.
The clever spider, weaved a beautiful web to catch her next meal.
The garden became a sanctuary for the spider, where it could construct its web undisturbed.

senses.definition: a place where many kinds of animals are kept for exhibition, breeding, and protection

examples.x: At the zoo, there was a fascinating reptile exhibit with snakes and lizards.
During our school trip, we visited the zoo and saw many different animals.
I love going to the zoo because I can see lions, tigers, and bears up close.
The zoo has a large enclosure for the elephants to roam freely.
We took photos of the colorful parrots at the zoo.

senses.definition: a large, wild animal with long legs which eats grass and can run very fast, typically the males have horns

examples.x: Driving through the national park, we were lucky enough to witness a herd of majestic deer.
In the early morning, I saw a group of deer prancing through the fields.
The deer cautiously approached the watering hole to quench its thirst.
The deer's fur blended perfectly with the autumn leaves, making it hard to spot.
We silently watched from a distance as the deer peacefully rested under the shade of a tree.

senses.definition: a large bird of prey with a sharp beak, long broad wings, and very good sight

examples.x: I wish I could see an eagle in the wild someday; they are such majestic creatures.
The eagle soared high in the sky, scanning the landscape for its next meal.
The eagle's keen eyesight allows it to spot prey from great distances.
We were amazed by the immense wingspan of the eagle as it flew overhead.
With its sharp talons, the eagle effortlessly caught a fish from the river.

senses.definition: a large, typically black insect, which has a hard case on its back that covers its wings

examples.x: As I lifted the rock, a shiny green beetle scurried away.
The beetle burrowed into the soft soil to create its underground home.
The beetle played a crucial role in the ecosystem by decomposing organic matter.
The beetle's hard exoskeleton provides protection from predators.
We saw a tiny beetle crawling on the leaves during our nature walk.

senses.definition: a black and yellow insect that collects nectar and produces wax and honey, which can fly and sting

examples.x: Bees are hardworking insects that tirelessly gather resources for their colony.
Bees communicate with each other through intricate dance movements.
The bee's black and yellow stripes serve as a warning to potential predators.
The sweet aroma of the blooming flowers attracted many bees.
We need to protect bees as they are essential for a healthy environment.

senses.definition: a large animal with sharp claws and thick fur, which eats meat, honey, insects, and fruits

examples.x: A bear has a thick fur coat to keep warm in cold weather.
I was really scared when I encountered a bear in the wilderness.
The bear is a symbol of strength and courage in many cultures.
The bear is known for its strong sense of smell.
We need to be careful when camping in bear territory.

senses.definition: a playful and intelligent animal that has a long tail and usually lives in trees and warm countries

examples.x: I saw the monkey hanging upside down from a tree branch.
I watched as the monkey interacted with other members of its troop.
My sister showed me the monkey eating a banana with delight.
The monkey's intelligence and adaptability made it a fascinating creature to study.
The monkey's long tail provided balance as it moved through the trees.

senses.definition: a flying insect with a long, thin body and large, typically brightly colored wings

examples.x: Butterflies are attracted to nectar-producing flowers for food.
In the butterfly exhibit, we saw various species from around the world.
The butterfly landed on my hand for a brief moment before taking flight again.
The butterfly's delicate wings featured intricate patterns and bright colors.
We learned that butterflies undergo a remarkable transformation from caterpillar to adult.

senses.definition: a flying insect that bites people and animals and feeds on their blood

examples.x: We used citronella candles to keep mosquitoes away during our outdoor picnic.

senses.definition: a small flying insect which is usually red with black spots

examples.x: Farmers appreciate ladybugs for their natural pest control abilities in the fields.
In the spring, ladybugs emerge from hibernation to find food.
Ladybugs are often considered good luck charms in many cultures.
The ladybug landed softly on a blade of grass after flying for a short distance.
The little girl giggled as the friendly ladybug crawled on her finger.

senses.definition: a large brown insect with a broad body, wings, long legs and antennae, considered a household pest

examples.x: Cockroaches are known for their ability to survive in various environments.
Cockroaches are nocturnal insects, often coming out at night.
I screamed when I saw a cockroach scurrying across the kitchen floor.
The cockroach quickly disappeared into a small crack in the wall.
We need to keep the kitchen clean to prevent cockroaches from coming in.

senses.definition: a flying insect with a soft body and a tail that shines in the dark

examples.x: As the sun set, we were delighted to see fireflies lighting up the night sky.
Fireflies are harmless and bring a sense of wonder to warm summer nights.
Fireflies create a magical display with their glowing lights in the summer evenings.
Fireflies use their light to communicate and attract mates.
Firefly populations thrive in areas with clean air and limited light pollution.

senses.definition: a class or group of people or things that have common characteristics or share particular qualities

examples.x: As a teacher, it's essential to understand the learning preferences of different types of students.
Environmentalists study the impact of pollution on different types of ecosystems.
In the animal kingdom, the lion belongs to the type of big cats.
The city is known for its diverse population, attracting people of various types from all over the world.
The museum displays art from various types of artists, both modern and classical.

senses.definition: (of an animal or plant) living or growing in a natural state, without any human interference

examples.x: During the camping trip, we heard the calls of wild birds echoing through the woods.
In the wilderness, you can encounter wild creatures like bears and wolves.
The dense jungle provides shelter for many wild creatures.
The safari allowed us to see wild elephants, zebras, and giraffes in their natural habitat.
We went on a hike through the wild forest, observing various animals and plants.

senses.definition: to pursue wild animals in order to kill or catch them, for sport or food

examples.x: The experienced tracker knew how to hunt stealthily without scaring away the animals.
The hunter patiently waited for the perfect opportunity to hunt the elusive deer.
The tribe used to hunt buffalo for their livelihood.

senses.definition: to sit on and control the movement of an animal, especially a horse

examples.x: As a beginner, I was a bit nervous to ride such a large horse.
During our vacation, we had the opportunity to ride horses along the scenic trails.
I would love to ride a donkey during our trip to the mountains.
The cowboys skillfully rode their horses as they herded cattle.

senses.definition: a group of people or things that have similar characteristics or share particular qualities

examples.x: At the museum, you can explore artifacts and relics from different historical kinds.
The farmer grew crops of different kinds, including wheat, corn, and potatoes.
The festival attracts people of different kinds, all celebrating together.
The restaurant's menu includes dishes from many kinds of cuisines, satisfying different tastes.
The store sells products of various kinds, from electronics to clothing.
